name: Story DoR — MF Servicing Extensions (Starter Draft)
description: Placeholder criteria for Multifamily Servicing stories. MF Servicing stewards should review and customize.
placeholder_note: Starter content. Replace with real MF Servicing DoR criteria.

criteria.name: Sub-Servicer Reach Stated
criteria.description: Which sub-servicers (of the active set) the story applies to is documented.
criteria.why: MF servicing operates across multiple sub-servicers with different capabilities; implicit scope causes silent gaps.
criteria.fix: List the sub-servicers in scope (and out of scope) and confirm capability with each named contact.
criteria.good_example: In scope: WellsFargo, KeyBank, Berkadia (covers ~85% of portfolio). Out of scope: Tier 2 sub-servicers (FEAT-MF-094 covers them).

criteria.name: Investor Reporting Impact Identified
criteria.description: Whether the change affects investor reporting (tape format, factor publication, remittance) is identified.
criteria.why: Servicing changes can ripple into investor reporting timing and accuracy — silent breaks affect investor trust.
criteria.fix: State the investor-reporting impact. If material, confirm Investor Reporting team has reviewed.
criteria.good_example: Affects Section 8 of the monthly tape (delinquency status). Reviewed with Investor Reporting team on [date].

criteria.name: Property Type Coverage Stated
criteria.description: Which property types (MF Conventional, Senior Housing, Healthcare, Affordable, etc.) the story applies to is stated.
criteria.why: MF property types have different servicing rules; assuming 'all MF' is rarely correct.
criteria.fix: List the property types in scope and out of scope, or attest 'applies to all MF property types'.
criteria.good_example: In scope: MF Conventional and Affordable. Out of scope: Senior Housing and Healthcare (different servicing rules — see FEAT-MF-101).
